chemistry.scope_summary: Single substrate photopinacol coupling of benzophenone using 2-propanol as H-atom donor and acetone as cosolvent in continuous flow reactor

batch_baseline.light_source: 1.5 dram vial with aluminum foil reflector

optimization_table.key_findings: Flow reaction achieved 95% conversion vs 5% for batch; average 82% conversion for flow vs 10% for batch

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: wrapped around the outside of the beaker

figures.flow_path: continuous flow through coiled tubing around light source
figures.reactor_geometry: coiled tubing around cylindrical light source
figures.key_information: Comparison of flow vs batch photoreduction setup; 0.2 M benzophenone in 2-propanol/acetone; residence time 10 min for flow; product is benzopinacol diol
figures.caption: Scheme 1. Photochemical Pinacol Coupling of Benzophenone

Figure 1.  Continuous  flow and batch setups for the photochemical pinacol coupling of benzophenone: (A) Complete  flow setup. (B) Flow setup without utility shade and aluminum foil reflector. (C) Batch setup from the side. (D) Batch setup looking into the utility shade. (E) Batch setup without utility shade or aluminum foil reflector.
figures.error: Error code: 400 - image exceeds 5 MB maximum

figures.caption: Figure 2.  Examples of   1 H NMR spectra of benzophenone prior to reaction (A) and the crude reaction mixture after the  flow reaction (B). The highlighted signals from starting material and from product were used to calculate reaction conversion.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Low conversion (5-10%) due to poor light penetration and mixing in static vial
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous mixing and optimized light exposure leading to 82-95% conversion
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction vessel geometry from static vial to continuous flow coil around light source
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Same substrate concentration (0.2 M), solvent system (2-propanol/acetone), and light wavelength (UVB)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Better heat dissipation and containment in flow system

figure_descriptions: Scheme 1: Reactor comparison diagram showing flow vs batch setup
Figure 1: Photographic comparison of complete experimental setups
Figure 2: NMR spectra for conversion analysis

notes: Educational paper comparing batch vs flow photochemistry for undergraduate instruction. High confidence in key performance metrics (yield improvement from 5% to 95%). Some experimental details missing but core comparison data is solid.
